revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ibaraki-jinja]][[ja:茨木神社]] {{Expand Japanese|茨木神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ibaraki Shrine | native_name = 茨木神社 | image = Ibaraki-jinja01s5s1980.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ibaraki Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.818576|135.570236}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|سوسانو|azb|سوسانو|bcl|Susanoo|be|Сусанаа|bg|Сусаноо|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|Σουσάνoο|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|سوسانو (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ibaraki Shrine'''|茨木神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province|ja|摂津国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 24 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039208|ltq=Q135039208|qid=Q135039208|1=Nihiyano- Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Sukukuno Shrine|lt=Sukukuno Shrine|qid=Q135039210|1=Sukukuno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q502403|ltq=Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} * {{ill|Q864163|ltq=Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Shimashimo district|lt=Shimashimo district|ja|島下郡|zh|岛下郡|qid=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.818576|135.570236}} == image (P18) == * Ibaraki-jinja01s5s1980.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|ar|سوسانو|azb|سوسانو|bcl|Susanoo|be|Сусанаа|bg|Сусаноо|ca|Susanoo|cs|Susanoo|da|Susa-no-o|de|Susanoo|el|Σουσάνoο|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|eo|Susanoo|es|Susanoo|eu|Susanoo|fa|سوسانو (خدا)|fi|Susanoo|fr|Susanoo|hr|Susano|hu|Szuszanoo|hy|Սուսանո|id|Susanoo|it|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|ka|სუსანოო-ნო-მიკოტო|ko|스사노오|la|Susano'o|lb|Susanoo|mni|ꯁꯨꯁꯅꯨ-ꯅꯣ-ꯃꯤꯀꯣꯇꯣ|nl|Susanoo|pl|Susanoo|pt|Susanoo|ro|Susanoo|ru|Сусаноо|simple|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|sl|Susanovo|sr|Сусаноо|su|Susanoo|sv|Susanoo|ta|சுசானவோ|th|ซูซาโนโอะ|tr|Susanoo|uk|Сусаноо|vi|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|zh_min_nan|Susanoo|zh|素戔嗚尊|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181044 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いばらきじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120905000114 == Commons category (P373) == * Ibaraki-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府茨木市元町4-3', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Ibaraki-jinja}} [[JA:茨木神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11617924}} == Japanese content == {{統合文字|茨}} {{神社 |名称 = Ibaraki Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ibaraki-jinja, haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibaraki|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}} Motomachi 4-3 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|qid=Q30016|1=807}}) |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|qid=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ibaraki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ibaraki Shrine''' (いばらきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ibaraki|Q502403|qid=Q502403|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. Within the precincts, the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine also stands. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity — {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Takehaya-Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * Associate deities — {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Honda Wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} === History === According to shrine tradition, the founding dates to {{ill|lt=Daidō|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=807|Q30016|qid=Q30016|1=807}}), when {{ill|lt=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro|Q941979|qid=Q941979|1=Sakanoue no Tamuramaro}} established the village of Ibarakiri no Sato (the origin of the place name "Ibaraki," said to be near present-day Miyamoto-chō); at that time, Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine—now the rear shrine (okumiya)—was founded. At that time, the shrine was located in what is now Miyamoto-chō, Ibaraki. The name Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine also appears in the "{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}." It is variously said that the shrine was moved to its present location when {{ill|lt=Kusunoki Masashige|Q382234|qid=Q382234|1=Kusunoki Masashige}} built {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Castle|Q11617856|qid=Q11617856|1=Ibaraki Castle}}, or that it moved in the late {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}. When it moved to the current site, {{ill|lt=Susanoo|Q272993|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|qid=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga Ōkami|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}} were jointly enshrined in the main hall with Ame-no-Iwatowake Ōkami, and {{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman Ōkami|1=Hachiman}} was enshrined in a separate building. The shrine received the reverence of successive castle lords ever since. During the {{ill|lt=Sengoku period|Q204023|qid=Q204023|1=Sengoku period}}, {{ill|lt=Nakagawa Kiyohide|Q6960145|qid=Q6960145|1=Nakagawa Kiyohide}}, lord of Ibaraki Castle, was a devout patron of the shrine. He erected a prohibition notice strictly forbidding lawless behavior against the shrine, and in {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1580|Q6712|qid=Q6712|1=1580}}) he donated 13 koku of shrine lands. The lord of {{ill|lt=Takatsuki Castle|Q11670758|qid=Q11670758|1=Takatsuki Castle}}, {{ill|lt=Takayama Ukon|Q749873|qid=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}}, protected {{ill|lt=Christianity|Q5043|qid=Q5043|1=Christianity}} while destroying neighboring temples and shrines. At that time, {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} issued an order that shrines enshrining {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, Kasuga Ōkami ({{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}), Hachiman Ōkami ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Honda Wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), and {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} (Susanoo)—Nobunaga's own {{ill|lt=ubusuna-gami|Q1392097|qid=Q1392097|1=Ubusunagami}}—must not be burned. Therefore, this shrine concealed the name Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine and styled itself "Gozu Tennō-sha" to escape destruction by Ukon (see also [https://web.archive.org/web/20020525192200/homepage.mac.com/ryomasuda/Saigoku/history/KamiGami/Goz3.html]). In {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|qid=Q635245|1=Genna}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1622|Q6815|qid=Q6815|1=1622}}), a new main hall was built to enshrine Gozu Tennō (Susanoo), Kasuga Ōkami (Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto), and Hachiman Ōkami (Honda Wake-no-Mikoto), and the former Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine became the rear shrine (okumiya). In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, but when parishioners began petitioning for shrine-rank authorization for Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine as well, in {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}} (Meiji 12) Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine was also ranked as a gōsha. This made it a nationally rare shrine with two gōsha within a single precinct. The following year, {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|qid=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13), a heiden and haiden were constructed, but they were replaced with new buildings in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|qid=Q49625|1=2022}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|qid=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 4). === Precinct === * Honden (main hall) — built in {{ill|lt=Genna|Q635245|qid=Q635245|1=Genna}} 8 ({{ill|lt=1622|Q6815|qid=Q6815|1=1622}}). Restored in {{ill|lt=2022|Q49625|qid=Q49625|1=2022}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|qid=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 4). * Heiden — rebuilt in 2022 (Reiwa 4). The previous building was demolished due to deterioration. * Haiden (worship hall) — rebuilt in 2022 (Reiwa 4). The previous building was demolished due to deterioration. * Plum garden — as one of the {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} Imperial Enthronement projects, approximately 50 red and white plum trees were planted in {{ill|lt=1991|Q2062|qid=Q2062|1=1991}} (Heisei 3). * Kuroi no Shimizu — together with Akai no Shimizu and Aoi no Shimizu, it has been one of the three famous springs of {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shimashimo District|1=UNKNOWN}} since ancient times. It is said to have been used for {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}'s tea ceremony as well. * Visitor rest house (Muharaki-sha) — built in February {{ill|lt=2011|Q1994|qid=Q1994|1=2011}} (Heisei 23). * East gate — when {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Castle|Q11617856|qid=Q11617856|1=Ibaraki Castle}} was abandoned in Genna 3 ({{ill|lt=1617|Q6798|qid=Q6798|1=1617}}), its karamete-mon (rear gate) was received and relocated here. * Assembly hall — built in 1991 (Heisei 3). The first floor houses the shrine office and mikoshi storehouse; the second floor is an assembly room. * Ceremonial hall — built in {{ill|lt=1966|Q2649|qid=Q2649|1=1966}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 41). The first floor is the saikan (purification hall); the second floor is a wedding hall. === Auxiliary shrines === * Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine (okumiya) — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} (Ame-no-Iwatowake Ōkami). Associate deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|Q462803|qid=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}, {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyokuni-no-Kami|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|lt=Tōshō-shin|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}. The original main shrine of this site and an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}. Ranked as {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in {{ill|lt=1879|Q7783|qid=Q7783|1=1879}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 12). Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style by parishioners and devotees in {{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|qid=Q2478|1=1974}} (Shōwa 49). * {{ill|lt=Kōtai Jingū|Q11581011|qid=Q11581011|1=Kōtai Jingū}} — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} — enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Uka-no-Mitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}}. Relocated in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|qid=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|qid=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|qid=Q713021|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Homusubi-no-Mikoto|1=Kagu-tsuchi}} * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Nushihara Shrine — enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|lt=Taga Shrine|Q11431089|qid=Q11431089|1=Taga shrine}} — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}. Relocated from Shimonakajō-mura in {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|qid=Q2056|1=1908}} (Meiji 41). * Kōtai Shrine — enshrined deity: Amaterasu Ōmikami * {{ill|lt=Kotohira Shrine|Q11646488|qid=Q11646488|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Kotohira shrines}} — enshrined deities: {{ill|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Sutoku|Q349287|qid=Q349287|1=Emperor Sutoku}}, {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko|Q4387856|qid=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|qid=Q191763|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} — enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|qid=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} — enshrined deities: {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}}. Relocated from a merchant household. File:Ibaraki-jinja, karihonden.jpg|Temporary main hall (as of 2021) File:Ibaraki-jinja, honden.jpg|Main hall (as of 2018) File:Ibaraki-jinja, Amenoiwatowake-jinja.jpg|Ame-no-Iwatowake Shrine (Shikinaisha) File:Ibaraki-jinja, higashimon.jpg|East gate (former Ibaraki Castle karamete-mon) File:Ibaraki-jinja, shoumen-torii.jpg|Front torii File:Ibaraki-jinja, higashi-torii.jpg|East torii === Major rites === * January 1: Saitan-sai * January 9–11: Tōka Ebisu Festival * February 11: Kigen-sai * April 8: Ningyō Hōshō-sai (doll burning ceremony) * April 18: Kinen-sai * June 30: Ōharae * July 13–14: Natsu-matsuri (summer festival) * October 10: Reitaisai (annual grand festival) * November 23: Niiname-sai * December 31: Ōharae === Access === * {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|qid=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}} ({{ill|lt=JR Kyoto Line|Q1195820|qid=Q1195820|1=JR Kyōto Line}}) {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Station|Q1072931|qid=Q1072931|1=Ibaraki Station}}, 700m east. * {{ill|lt=Hankyū Kyoto Main Line|Q2053737|qid=Q2053737|lt=Hankyū Railway Kyoto Line|1=Hankyu Kyoto Main Line}} {{ill|lt=Ibaraki-shi Station|Q3969806|qid=Q3969806|1=Ibaraki-shi Station}}, 600m west. The Ibaraki City Hall is diagonally across the intersection and can serve as a landmark. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commons|Category:Ibaraki-jinja}} * [http://www.ibarakijinja.or.jp/ Ibaraki Shrine official site] * [https://web.archive.org/web/20020525192200/homepage.mac.com/ryomasuda/Saigoku/history/KamiGami/Goz3.html Deities of Senri — Gozu Tennō / Nobunaga Defense Theory] * [http://www.baika.ac.jp/~publisher-bookstore/ibaraki/kakumoto04/index.htm This and That of Ibaraki Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{pref-stub|pref=Osaka Prefecture}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ibaraki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ibaraki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ibaraki Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:祇園神社]]
